generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalAirTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors internal air temperature within the incubator's insulated container. Provides real-time data inputs for thermal simulation models to enable calibration and validation of thermal inertia parameters."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance InternalContentTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures temperature of contents inside the incubator's insulated container. Supports thermal uniformity validation and model calibration for dynamic thermal response."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks external ambient temperature conditions to provide environmental context for thermal model validation and adaptive control strategies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalAirTemperatureSensor, InternalContentTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data from internal sensors transmitted to the Digital Twin for real-time calibration and monitoring."
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Ambient temperature data transmitted to the Digital Twin for environmental context in thermal simulations."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal simulation model with two free parameters representing simplified thermal inertia characteristics of the incubator's heatbed."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity thermal simulation model with four free parameters demonstrating superior accuracy in predicting thermal inertia and dynamic response."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed internal temperature measurements from both air and content sensors, used for model calibration and state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, RealTimeMonitoringEnabler, FaultDetectionEnabler
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed ambient temperature data providing environmental context for thermal model validation and adaptive control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance ThermalModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calibrated thermal model parameters (thermal inertia, time constants) updated in real-time through data-driven optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Parameter estimation algorithms (least-squares optimization, Bayesian inference) that update model parameters using real-time sensor data to maintain alignment with Physical Twin behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveSimulationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation engine (Python/SciPy or MATLAB/Simulink) executing differential equation models to predict thermal behavior under current or hypothetical conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService, OptimizationRecommendationService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithms processing sensor data to provide continuous assessment of Physical Twin performance and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory algorithms identifying safety violations or operational deviations by comparing model predictions with real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithms (PID, MPC) generating adaptive control signals for heatbed/fan operation to maintain temperature stability while minimizing energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationRecommendationService, DynamicReconfigurationService
]

instance DataVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard tools (Grafana, MATLAB Dashboards) providing interactive visualizations of system states, alerts, and predictive analytics for engineering communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdvancedVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition and processing of sensor data to enable state estimation and performance assessment of the Physical Twin's thermal regulation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalStateInsight, SystemPerformanceInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of dynamic thermal models to reproduce and predict Physical Twin behavior under current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalResponsePredictionInsight, FailureRiskAssessmentInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identification of deviations from optimal performance through comparison of model predictions with real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, SafetyViolationAlert
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of adaptive control signals for heatbed and fan operation to maintain temperature stability while optimizing energy efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlSignalAction, EnergyOptimizationInsight
]

instance AdvancedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of interactive visualizations including time-series plots and 3D thermal maps to support engineering communication and decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalVisualizationInsight, SystemStateDashboard
]

instance DynamicReconfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Adaptation to changing environmental conditions through runtime model updates and control parameter adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides ReconfigurationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ThermalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of internal and external temperature states with confidence measures derived from model calibration accuracy."
]

instance SystemPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time evaluation of incubator's thermal regulation performance against operational targets."
]

instance ThermalResponsePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted thermal behavior under current or hypothetical conditions including temperature drift predictions."
]

instance FailureRiskAssessmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted risks of component failures (e.g., heater degradation, insulation breaches) based on model simulations."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified deviations from optimal performance including sensor failures or insulation breaches."
]

instance SafetyViolationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered alert when safety thresholds are exceeded, requiring immediate corrective action."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlSignalAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically generated actuation commands for heatbed and fan operation based on optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ReconfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters or model selection based on changing environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified opportunities for energy consumption reduction while maintaining temperature stability."
]

instance ThermalVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visual representations of temperature distributions and thermal gradients within the incubator."
]

instance SystemStateDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive overview of current system states including temperature readings, model predictions, and alert statuses."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electrical heating element responsible for generating and distributing thermal energy inside the incubator's insulated container."
]

instance AirCirculationFan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical fan ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the incubator by circulating air."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Central embedded controller interfacing between Physical Twin sensors and actuators, executing real-time monitoring and control logic."
]

// C1: Physical System Components
instance InsulatedContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to maintain internal temperature stability regardless of external conditions."
]

instance IncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System-based incubator designed for temperature regulation with closed-loop control requirements."
    base:contains InsulatedContainer, HeatbedActuator, AirCirculationFan, InternalAirTemperatureSensor,
                InternalContentTemperatureSensor, ExternalAmbientTemperatureSensor, RaspberryPiController
]

instance DigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Modular Digital Twin architecture integrating models, algorithms, and feedback loops for thermal regulation of the incubator."
    base:contains TwoParameterThermalModel, FourParameterThermalModel, ThermalModelParameters,
                InternalTemperatureData, ExternalTemperatureData,
                ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler,
                RealTimeMonitoringEnabler, FaultDetectionEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler,
                DataVisualizationEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via **command signals generated by supervisory algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, optimizers, or state estimators) running on the cyber layer. The **control flow** originates from the DT’s **optimization and monitoring modules**, which analyze the calibrated CPS models and sensor data to determine **actuation commands** for the PT. These commands are transmitted to the **Raspberry Pi controller**, which interfaces with the **heatbed** (heating element) and **fan** (air distribution) to regulate the incubator’s internal temperature. The documents do not specify **exact command types** (e.g., PWM signals for heatbed, binary on/off for fan) or **message formats**, but the implied **closed-loop architecture** suggests **digital control signals** (e.g., duty-cycle modulation for heating). **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the **calibration algorithms** (Section 3, Document 2/3) imply a form of **model-based validation** to ensure commands align with PT constraints. **Feedback systems** are inherent to the DT’s design, as the PT’s sensor data is continuously looped back to the DT for **real-time correction** of control actions. **Conflict resolution** is not detailed, but the **supervisory controller** (as referenced in Document 1) likely prioritizes safety-critical commands (e.g., emergency cooling) over non-critical adjustments. **Command execution confirmation** is implied via the **monitoring loop**, where the DT cross-references sensor feedback against expected outcomes to validate PT response."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study relies on **real-time synchronization** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its Digital Twin (DT) via **sensor data collection** (temperature sensors inside and outside the insulated container) and **control feedback loops** managed by a Raspberry Pi. The temporal granularity is dictated by the **sampling rate of the temperature sensors**, which is not explicitly quantified in the documents but is implied to be sufficiently frequent to enable **dynamic model calibration** and **predictive control** (e.g., fault detection, state estimation, or supervisory adjustments). The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment in near-real-time, ensuring the DT reflects the PT’s current operating conditions. Latency requirements are inferred to be **low** to maintain alignment between the PT’s state (e.g., temperature dynamics) and the DT’s simulations, particularly for safety-critical adjustments (e.g., heating/cooling interventions). The use of **differential equation-based dynamic models** (e.g., for thermal behavior) suggests a need for **high-fidelity temporal resolution** during simulation, though trade-offs between accuracy and computational speed are acknowledged (e.g., slower simulations for more precise models)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)-based architecture**, as depicted in Figure 1, where the DT integrates **three core components**:
  1. **Data Layer**: Real-time sensor data (temperature) from the PT, collected via **embedded sensors** (two internal, one external) and transmitted to the DT for **state estimation**.
  2. **Model Layer**: **Differential equation-based dynamic models** (e.g., thermal physics) and **calibrated abstractions** of the PT’s cyber (Raspberry Pi control logic) and physical (heatbed, fan, insulation) subsystems. Models operate at **multiple abstraction levels**, balancing accuracy and simulation speed.
  3. **Algorithm Layer**: **Calibration algorithms** (e.g., parameter tuning for thermal models), **fault detectors**, **supervisory controllers**, and **optimizers** that process data to generate actionable insights (e.g., adjusting heating power). The **orchestration** is **closed-loop**: calibrated models inform monitors (safety/optimality checks), which trigger **human or algorithmic interventions** (e.g., Raspberry Pi adjustments) to the PT.
  The system lacks explicit **distributed orchestration** (e.g., microservices, service meshes) or **resource management** details but implies **tight coupling** between components. Integration patterns are **internal** (PT ↔ DT), with no mention of **external orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, edge computing)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle stages in the provided context include **pre-deployment modeling and simulation phases**, where abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for physical attributes like wheel sizes or chassis shapes) and dynamic models (e.g., differential equations for system behavior analysis) are developed. These models are later integrated into the DT framework to enable real-time monitoring and predictive capabilities. During **deployment**, the DT continuously collects **sensor data** from the Physical Twin (PT) to update its models via **calibration algorithms**, ensuring alignment between the virtual and physical systems. Post-deployment, the DT operates in a **closed-loop feedback system**, where **monitors** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) assess system performance against predefined safety and optimality criteria. Violations trigger **corrective actions**, either automated (via algorithms) or human-driven, to adjust the PT dynamically. The lifecycle also emphasizes **model retention and evolution**, as models are not discarded post-deployment but iteratively refined using real-time data to maintain accuracy and relevance. Governance is implicit in the integration of **optimization algorithms** and **supervisory controllers**, which enforce operational constraints and adaptive decision-making throughout the system's lifecycle. The transition between phases is managed via **data-driven model calibration** and **algorithm-mediated feedback loops**, ensuring seamless adaptation to environmental or operational changes in the PT."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) emphasizes **model fidelity** through **validation and verification (V&V) methods**, as demonstrated in the incubator case study. The **thermal simulation models** were evaluated based on their ability to predict **thermal inertia**, with the **four-parameter model** selected for its higher accuracy. **Uncertainty sources** include discrepancies between model assumptions and real-world behavior, addressed via **calibration algorithms** that adjust model parameters using real-time sensor data. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are implied through the DT's **monitoring systems**, which identify deviations (e.g., unsafe or suboptimal performance) and trigger corrective actions (e.g., human or algorithmic interventions). **Quality assurance** is ensured by iterative model refinement, leveraging data-driven updates and comparative analysis (e.g., two-parameter vs. four-parameter models). The documents highlight the need for **continuous validation** to maintain model alignment with the Physical Twin (PT) under varying operating conditions."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) of the incubator relies on a **cyber-physical system (CPS)-based network infrastructure**, where the **Raspberry Pi** serves as the primary **edge computing node** bridging the PT and DT. While **specific communication protocols** (e.g., Ethernet, Wi-Fi, or serial protocols like UART) are not explicitly stated, the implied architecture suggests **low-latency, high-reliability links** for real-time sensor-actuator communication. The **network topology** is likely **star-shaped**, with the Raspberry Pi as the central hub collecting sensor data and distributing control commands. **Bandwidth requirements** are minimal but critical for **temporal synchronization**, as the DT’s predictive and monitoring capabilities depend on **near-real-time data streams**. **Latency constraints** are not quantified, but the use of **calibration algorithms** (Document 2/3, Section 3) implies that delays must be short enough to avoid divergence between the PT and DT models. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not described, though the **closed-loop control system** suggests fault tolerance via **feedback-driven corrections**. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, but the integration of **supervisory controllers** (Document 1) implies basic **authentication and integrity checks** to prevent unauthorized command injection or data tampering."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) for the incubator is deployed on a **hybrid infrastructure**, primarily leveraging **edge computing** (via the Raspberry Pi) for real-time processing and **local storage** of sensor data and models. The documents do not specify **cloud hosting**, but the **scalability mechanisms** are implied to be **lightweight**, given the simplicity of the use case. **Computational requirements** are minimal, focusing on **model calibration** (e.g., solving differential equations for thermal dynamics) and **real-time monitoring** (e.g., threshold-based alerts). **Storage requirements** are similarly modest, storing only **time-series sensor data** and **model parameters** for the CPS. **Deployment models** are not explicitly detailed, but the **Raspberry Pi’s embedded nature** suggests a **self-contained, on-premise deployment** with no reliance on external cloud services. **Availability and reliability assurance** are not quantified, though the **closed-loop control system** and **calibration algorithms** imply **high resilience** to transient failures (e.g., sensor drops) via **model-based recovery**. The **publicly available repository** (Document 2/3) suggests an **open-source, reproducible deployment**, but no specifics on **containerization (e.g., Docker)** or **orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes)** are provided."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system integrates **cybersecurity protocols** and **safety-critical mechanisms** to ensure reliable operation of the Physical Twin (PT). The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation and mitigating deviations from expected behavior. **Monitors** within the DT system continuously check for safe and optimal performance, detecting violations that may indicate security threats (e.g., unauthorized access, sensor tampering) or safety risks (e.g., temperature anomalies in the incubator case study). Fail-safe mechanisms are implied through the **supervisory controllers** and **optimizers**, which intervene when deviations are detected, either through human intervention or automated corrective actions. The system leverages **access control mechanisms** (e.g., authentication for model updates and monitoring access) to restrict unauthorized modifications to the DT’s algorithms or data streams. Risk assessment is embedded in the **monitoring phase**, where real-time data from sensors (e.g., temperature sensors in the incubator) is cross-referenced with calibrated models to preemptively identify and mitigate risks. The DT’s architecture inherently supports **safety-critical system considerations** by maintaining a closed-loop between the PT and its virtual counterpart, ensuring alignment between physical and digital states."
]
